This page explains, in simple terms, how this website stores small bits of data in your browser.

## What are cookies and local storage?

Cookies and local storage are browser features that let a website save small pieces of data.
Cookies are small text files saved by a website, while local storage keeps data directly in your browser.
Both help the site remember settings and support basic features.

## What this website may store

This theme can store small technical settings, for example:

- Your theme preference (light, dark, or system)
- Your language preference
- Cached search data to make search faster

This data is used for site features only, not to identify you personally.

## Third-party content

If a page includes third-party content (for example maps or embedded videos), those services may set their own cookies.
Those cookies are controlled by the third-party provider, not by this theme.

## How to manage or delete this data

You can clear cookies and local storage in your browser settings at any time.
If you clear this data, some preferences may reset.

You can also use the button below to remove all data stored by this site in your browser immediately.

{% include storage-widget.html %}

## Questions

If you have questions about how this website uses browser storage, contact the site owner.
